ground_truth.title: How to book flights when moving to another country?
ground_truth.author: drat
ground_truth.main_content:
I am planning to move to a new country this autumn. (In my case from Switzerland to Singapore) However I am not sure, which is the most cost-saving way of buying my air tickets. I can think of the following alternatives: Buying a one-way ticket. This would obviously be the easiest method, but then in the past I made the experience, that this is more expensive. Buying a return ticket with a flexible return date. I could then use the return ticket for a home-holiday. However that sounds like an organisation nightmare, since then I'd have to do the same for the next return flight etc. Buying a return ticket and then get a refund for the return (Is that even possible?). I would be interested to hear how other people have tackled this problem. 

Answer highlights: If you're talking long-haul, then round trip is in most cases cheaper than one way, and significantly so. Exception from the rule I've stumbled upon was Aer Lingus, which has long-haul flight with one-way cheaper than return tickets. As for getting a refund for unused return leg, your ticket must be bought with (semi-)flexible fare, which cost significantly more than the cheapest one. 

I've moved countries several times now, and apart from when I knew I was only going to be in the US for two 3-month stints, so the return ticket was obvious, the other times I've always bought one-way tickets. It's always been cheaper, even if only slightly, and it frees you up - if you have a return ticket, you also don't feel like you're really committing to that other country. 

For the first time I am leaving home, I bought the cheapest one-way ticket I found, it was a 2 stops ticket. Do not buy a round trip ticket with a return in the far future. This will usually cost more. When I want to go back home, I bought the cheapest round trip ticket, usually I stayed for short periods at home which allowed me to find very cheap tickets.
with: I am planning to move to a new country this autumn. (In my case from Switzerland to Singapore) However I am not sure, which is the most cost-saving way of buying my air tickets.

If you're talking long-haul, then round trip is in most cases cheaper than one way, and significantly so (eg. fares I've checked recently would be $1000 round-trip vs $3500 just the first leg of the exactly the same trip with same carrier).

As for getting a refund for unused return leg of the trip, the issue is that in order to be able to do that, your ticket must be bought with (semi-)flexible fare, which cost significantly more than the cheapest one.
I've moved countries several times now, and the other times I've always bought one-way tickets. It's always been cheaper, even if only slightly, and it frees you up.
For the first time I am leaving home, I bought the cheapest one-way ticket I found. Do not buy a round trip ticket with a return in the far future. This will usually cost more.
